name: AI Avatar Talking-Head
description: Fully AI-generated 'person' delivering a 45-90s script straight to camera. In 2026, lip-sync (Kling Avatar v2, HeyGen, Synthesia) reached the point where the average TikTok viewer doesn't immediately spot AI; one TikTok Shop affiliate earned ~$13K commission on a single AI-avatar video. Solves the biggest UGC bottleneck — production cost — and unlocks one-script-to-N-markets via ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 (30+ languages, voice preserved). Close-up persona delivers hook → value → CTA, with optional B-roll cutaways. AI = accelerator, not replacement: the viewer must NOT feel they're watching slop. Platform-side C2PA provenance metadata is emitted automatically by render with genai:true; no visible on-screen disclosure overlay (retention drops with visible AI labels).

stackSummary.personaKeyframe: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview via OpenRouter — one canonical avatar character keyframe per project, reused across episodes for stability. Use multi-ref WITH a persona reference image when consistency across episodes matters (strongly recommended for any series / brand presenter).
stackSummary.talkingHeadVideo: google/veo-3.1-fast (DEFAULT) via OpenRouter — model-native audio with `--audio` and image-conditioned generation in one call gives TikTok-grade lip-sync in 2026. 4-6-8s clips, ~$0.25/clip. Image-to-video with the persona keyframe as the start frame keeps the avatar consistent across scenes. CRITICAL: `generate_audio: true` is only clean for English and Chinese — for Russian / Ukrainian / other languages, generate audio separately via ElevenLabs and render the avatar with `generate_audio: false` (it produces silent video with mouth motion driven by the audio passed via OpenRouter's lipsync conditioning).
stackSummary.premiumTier: google/veo-3.1 (full) — reserve for hero spots where 4K mastering or top-tier lip-sync fidelity is the entire point. ~$0.50/clip. veo-3.1-fast is sufficient for almost every TikTok-grade use case.
stackSummary.budgetTier: google/veo-3.1-lite — ~$0.15/clip for high-volume multilingual batch (10+ language regen per episode). Lip-sync quality steps down but stays acceptable for educational / news brief tone.
stackSummary.bRoll: kwaivgi/kling-v3.0-pro — optional 3-5s cutaways (product close-ups, screen recordings, illustrative shots). generate_audio: false; the persona audio carries throughout.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — single TTS call per language. For multilingual exports, regen VO N times (one per language) and recompose with veo-3.1-fast per language using the same persona keyframe. Voice settings: stability 0.40, similarity_boost 0.75, style 0.15 (warm, authoritative).
stackSummary.captions: OpenRouter whisper-1 → MinimalCaptions or KaraokeCaptions overlay. HormoziCaptions only when the script demands punch (e.g. e-commerce hard sell).
stackSummary.music: Optional. ElevenLabs Music low-volume bed (-18 dB under VO) when the script is review/ad. Educational and news briefs usually go music-free.

constants.personaStability: single canonical keyframe reused per project — never regen mid-series
constants.disclosurePolicy: C2PA provenance metadata only (emitted by render with genai:true); no on-screen overlay

variationAxes: avatar archetype (8 archetypes — see workspace/personas/ARCHETYPES.md): friendly-creator, expert-educator, news-anchor, gen-z-streetwear, corporate-presenter, indie-founder, lifestyle-coach, gamer-reviewer
language (English / Russian / Spanish / Portuguese / Hindi / Arabic / Mandarin / Japanese / French / German — anywhere ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 supports)
use case (product review / educational explainer / news brief / e-commerce ad / pitch / personal essay)
background (solid color / minimal room / softly blurred environment / on-brand backdrop)
B-roll discipline (talking-head only / 1-2 cutaways / cutaway-heavy hybrid)
doNotCopyLiterally: Avatar likeness, voice, exact script, brand identity. Vibe + structure only. Never reproduce a real public figure's likeness or voice.
referenceNotes: Reference is OPTIONAL but STRONGLY RECOMMENDED. The reference-required gate (AGENTS.md hard rule #3) does NOT trigger for fictional AI personas — but persona stability across episodes does require a canonical keyframe. Recommended workflow: (1) on episode 1, generate the avatar from an archetype prompt OR a user-supplied persona reference; save the resulting keyframe to workspace/projects/<id>/assets/persona/avatar.png and treat it as the project's reference for every subsequent episode and language render. (2) For brand presenters, the user MUST supply a persona reference (a real human face the brand has rights to, or a previously-generated avatar). (3) NEVER use a real named person's likeness without explicit signed consent — that's the reference-required gate firing for the wrong reason and refusing is correct. See AGENTS.md and (when present) workspace/personas/ARCHETYPES.md for the 8 archetype options.
